finding_results.notes: Five revisions across two scenes. (1) Yusuf's aphorism 'don't confuse it with punishment' replaced with tired, fragmented Birmingham-register speech that conveys the same reassurance through technical framing ('That's just the gap, yeah? The system was filling all of that in'). (2) Lena's composed 'taking time I was alive for' replaced with rougher experiential description including self-interruption ('I didn't — I didn't know how I got there. Minutes just gone'). (3) The quotable X-versus-Y contrast 'keeping me in line / taking over the line' replaced with flat experiential statement ('Because I wasn't swimming... The stroke was there. I wasn't'), with Dara anchoring to the concrete cost ('That's five seconds, Lena'). (4) Narrator certification paragraph ('The question landed... heavy and not theatrical') replaced with one-line physical beat ('Water lapped the gutter between them'). (5) Abstract meta-label exchange ('Bad timing for philosophy' / 'This isn't philosophy') replaced with grounded pool-partner speech ('Eat something' / 'That's not going to change the answer'). All three required facts preserved: Lena lost conscious time (line 479), the patch cost five seconds at easy pace (lines 473, 501), and Lena would still choose it (lines 517-519, 535).

summary: Roughened five composed-thematic passages across the Yusuf departure and Dara pool exchange. Replaced Yusuf's clean aphorism with tired Birmingham fragments, Lena's balanced contrast line with flat experiential speech plus self-correction, and Dara's meta-label with concrete training-partner dialogue. Removed narrator certification of dialogue weight. All three acceptance-test facts (lost time, five-second cost, would choose again) still arrive through scene-pressured speech and physical business. No continuity, heading, or structural changes.
